steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 0,4 N...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 0,4 N HCl by soft pipetting until no clumps are left in solution ( 125 µL for 1x10 6 cells ) If necessary, vortex softly

steps.title: Incubate 4h in acid on rotator...
steps.instruction: Incubate 4h in acid on rotator 4°C to promote lysis of nuclei and solubilization of histones.

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to new Eppendorfs
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to new Eppendorfs. Histones are present in the acid since they are alkaline proteins

steps.title: Add, drop by drop (very slowly...
steps.instruction: Add, drop by drop (very slowly and in the middle of the eppendorf), TCA ( final concentration 33%: 61.25µl for 1x10 6 ) to promote precipitation of histones and invert the tube several times.

steps.title: Spin for 10 min at 4°C,...
steps.instruction: Spin for 10 min at 4°C, 16000g to pellet the histones. Important: Place all the eppendorfs in the same position => makes it easier to know where the pellet is localized

steps.title: Remove the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant. Be careful, the pellet is not always visible, smear on wall.

steps.title: Add ±150 µL ice-cold acetone (don’t...
steps.instruction: Add ±150 µL ice-cold acetone (don’t resuspend the pellet) to remove TCA.

steps.title: Add ±150 µL cold acetone (don’t...
steps.instruction: Add ±150 µL cold acetone (don’t resuspend the pellet) to remove TCA. If not all the acid is removed it will destroy the SDS-gel.

steps.title: Dry at room temperature for 30...
steps.instruction: Dry at room temperature for 30 minutes (until no acetone left).

steps.title: Resuspend in milliQ water ( 50...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in milliQ water ( 50 µL for 1x10 6 cells ).

steps.title: Transfer 20 µL (from 400
steps.instruction: Transfer 20 µL (from 400.000 cells) to a new Eppendorf tube for gel.
